Description.SourceString:
I despise the rambling of seers. Viika persists with their inscrutable announcements of something sinister to come yet lacks the ability to make their meaning clear. Lacrussa fusses over their sibling as they have their little meltdown while irritating me with their lack of clarity. 

Precision is important. Vagueness helps no one and causes more harm than good. Seers never understood this. I remember speaking to Kranon once, long before his fall, and he laughed with me about the eccentricities of the timeswept. He explained how they exist in moments of their lives other than the ones we are bound to, lost in eddies of temporal currents while the rest of us are anchored in place. 

It seems like a cruel existence, to be certain, but that does not make up for the frustration they create. 

What do they mean when they say, “the blood makes her hunger”? What does that even mean? Who are these “warriors from across the sea” that will “put an end to her vengeance”. Who is “her”? Who are they? What in creation is the seer talking about?

Enough. I’m done. I leave Lacrussa to her sibling’s nonsense. I have more pressing things to do.
